---
name: mock-to-live-validation
description: Validate the transition of a data layer from MOCK to LIVE status. Required before any layer status change.
---

# Mock-to-Live Data Transition Validation

## Purpose
Ensure data integrity and schema compatibility when switching any GIS layer from mock data to a live API endpoint. Prevents broken maps, missing attributes, and silent data corruption.

## Trigger Condition
Invoke before switching ANY layer from MOCK to LIVE status.

## Procedure

### Step 1 — Pre-Transition Checklist
Confirm all prerequisites:
- [ ] Endpoint URL is CONFIRMED in `docs/API_STATUS.md`
- [ ] Response format is documented (GeoJSON / ArcGIS REST JSON / MVT)
- [ ] Field mapping between mock and live schemas exists
- [ ] `api_cache` table schema can accommodate this data
- [ ] Mock GeoJSON schema matches live endpoint schema

### Step 2 — Endpoint Verification
Test the live endpoint:
1. HTTP GET to service directory → expect 200
2. Test query with Cape Town CBD bounding box: `{ west: 18.4, south: -33.95, east: 18.5, north: -33.9 }`
3. Check for auth requirements (401/403 → document and configure)
4. Measure response time (budget: <2s for initial load)
5. Update `docs/API_STATUS.md` with results

### Step 3 — Field Mapping Validation
Compare every field in the live response to documentation:
- Flag `DATA_GAP` for expected fields missing from live response
- Flag `UNDOCUMENTED_FIELD` for live fields not in documentation
- Verify data types match (string vs number, date formats)

### Step 4 — Data Quality Checks
Sample 3+ suburbs (e.g. Woodstock, Camps Bay, Khayelitsha):
- [ ] Coordinates are EPSG:4326 and render correctly on MapLibre
- [ ] Zone codes match IZS classification (if applicable)
- [ ] No null geometry features
- [ ] Feature count within performance limits (<10,000 per layer for GeoJSON)
- [ ] Attribute values are reasonable (no placeholder text, no test data)

### Step 5 — Badge Update
- Switch layer status to LIVE in `DataSourceBadge`
- Confirm badge displays `[SOURCE_NAME · YEAR · LIVE]`
- Confirm mock file is no longer loaded for this layer
- Confirm cache-first behaviour: subsequent loads hit `api_cache`

### Step 6 — Rollback Plan
Document the rollback procedure:
1. Switch layer status back to CACHED or MOCK
2. Update `DataSourceBadge` accordingly
3. Log failure reason in `docs/API_STATUS.md`
4. No data loss — cached data preserved in `api_cache`

## When NOT to Use This Skill
- Basemap tile transitions (separate tile infrastructure)
- Internal data generated by the application (saved searches, favourites)
- Data that never had a mock stage
